parts.title: Part 5 – Incomplete Sentences
parts.description: Read each sentence. You will notice that there is a word or phrase missing. Study the four answer choices and select the one answer: (A), (B), (C), or (D), that best completes the sentence.

questions.question_text: Everyone is welcome to walk around the exhibit hall, but visitors are _____ from eating or drinking.
questions.explanation: Only 'discouraged' (the opposite of encouraged) is followed by 'from' and fits the meaning of this sentence.

questions.question_text: Unless we fix the gas leak soon, we run the risk of the situation becoming so serious that we _____ have to evacuate the production site.
questions.explanation: We use the modal verb 'will' for prediction in this context.

questions.question_text: If you have any further questions, please contact our reception staff who will be glad to _____ further assistance.
questions.explanation: In this case, 'provide' collocates with 'assistance'.

questions.question_text: Negotiations between management and workers over the new _____ contract were finally concluded at a meeting with union leaders last night.
questions.explanation: The compound noun 'employment contract' is used here.

questions.question_text: The results of the market research survey _____ that the reputation of the company had suffered due to negative press reports earlier in the year.
questions.explanation: To 'confirm' means to verify or substantiate.

questions.question_text: By the time the conference was finally over, everyone was so tired that they were _____ asleep on their feet.
questions.explanation: Here 'practically' (meaning nearly/almost) fits the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question_text: Customers who are not satisfied with any of our products may bring them back at any time and _____ them for an alternative of their choice.
questions.explanation: The verb used here is 'exchange' (something for something else).

questions.question_text: We have never had a single accident in the history of the company, _____ is an industrial safety record we are very proud of.
questions.explanation: Only this non-defining relative pronoun can be used here.

questions.question_text: One thing that was very noticeable about the debate was the great _____ of opinions expressed by the participants.
questions.explanation: Only the noun 'diversity' (meaning variety) describes 'opinions'.

questions.question_text: Due to the upturn in the global economy, sales have risen _____ over the past two months.
questions.explanation: The adverb 'sharply' collocates with the verb 'rise'.

questions.question_text: Increasing wages for the entire staff equally is a very fair and cost-effective way for a company to motivate _____ employees and boost performance.
questions.explanation: Here the possessive adjective refers to the singular subject 'company'.

questions.question_text: Campers in the National Park are welcome to hike anywhere they want, but fishing in the lakes and rivers is not _____ in order to protect the balance of our wildlife population.
questions.explanation: Only the -ed form of the adjective completes this sentence.

questions.question_text: I know the negotiations were very difficult, but I would not have agreed to such a big discount, if I _____ you.
questions.explanation: The phrase used here is 'if I were you'.

questions.question_text: We believe the secret to the success of every one of our projects comes down to good design and thorough _____.
questions.explanation: This noun collocates with 'thorough' and fits the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question_text: After a long delay due to bad weather, our flight _____ London to Vancouver finally took off six hours behind schedule.
questions.explanation: The structure used here is 'from … to'.

questions.question_text: An electrician was called to check the smoke alarm, which had gone off several times in the office building _____ there was no smoke.
questions.explanation: Only this conjunction showing contrast fits here.

questions.question_text: Mr. Arakawa was chosen to lead the project because he was one of the most hard working and _____ members of the team.
questions.explanation: This adjective is needed to describe the subject, Mr. Arakawa.

questions.question_text: When Mike and Claude became business partners they knew that in order for the company to succeed they would have to trust _____ completely.
questions.explanation: The collocation used here is 'each other' (one another).

questions.question_text: Following the first night's performance of the new play, all of the reviews were, without _____, full of praise.
questions.explanation: The collocation used here is 'without exception'.

questions.question_text: Please can you pass _____ the blue folder over there on the top shelf?
questions.explanation: The object pronoun completes the phrase 'pass someone something'.

questions.question_text: Although our stand at the exhibition was not very big, the event went very well for us because far more potential customers _____ up than we had expected.
questions.explanation: Here, only 'showed' can be followed by 'up' ('show up' means arrive).

questions.question_text: The report concluded that the heavy losses the company sustained over the previous year meant that _____ could have been done to save it from bankruptcy.
questions.explanation: Only 'nothing' fits the sense of this sentence.

questions.question_text: After Roberto was promoted to supervisor in the packing department, he _____ given the freedom necessary to change procedures.
questions.explanation: The past simple passive completes this past action.

questions.question_text: The furniture store drastically cut its prices across its entire line of luxury sofas _____ an attempt to generate more sales.
questions.explanation: This preposition completes the phrase 'in an attempt to'.

questions.question_text: The coffee maker that Zoe gave us as a wedding present is _____ better than the last one we had, isn't it?
questions.explanation: Only 'much' can be used to intensify the comparative 'better than'.

questions.question_text: The annual 'Best in Business' award ceremony, which is the most prestigious event of the year, _____ take place in the Grosvenor Center on Saturday, June 3rd.
questions.explanation: The future simple is used here for a future event.

questions.question_text: I was surprised at all the positive comments I received after my talk, because I never expected it _____ so well.
questions.explanation: Here, the infinitive with 'to' is used after the verb 'expect'.

questions.question_text: Our enjoyment of the fishing expedition was severely _____ by the bad weather, which caused several members of our group to be seasick.
questions.explanation: The verb 'to be affected' (meaning influenced) + 'by' is used here.

questions.question_text: The meal at the restaurant downtown was so expensive that we decided to share the cost, and so _____ of us paid $25.
questions.explanation: Here the pronoun 'each' is followed by 'of'.

questions.question_text: Lindsey Martinez, the Director of Operations at Hope Hospital, said the growing number of patients suffering from tropical disease was _____ alarming.
questions.explanation: Here, only this adverb can describe the adjective 'alarming'.

parts.title: Part 6 – Text Completion
parts.description: Read each text. You will notice that there are four blanks. For each blank, study the four answer choices and select the one answer: (A), (B), (C), or (D), that best completes the text.

question_groups.passage_content:
To: Kirsty Spalding, Reservations Manager – Hotel Grand Chapelle
From: Rei Matsumoto
Subject: Room confirmation

Dear Ms. Spalding,
Regarding our recent telephone conversation, I am writing to confirm the following reservation at your hotel: One non-smoking executive double room, for two nights beginning Friday, May 29.

Can you please confirm that the room will have a balcony with a [131] of the sea, as promised? We would also like a room with a bathtub, rather than a shower, if at all possible.

We understand that the room rate of 120 per night includes breakfast. Can you also confirm that the hotel provides free internet access, and that the room [132] a safe?

We understand that no deposit is required, but we need to make payment in full on arrival. [133]

Thank you for your time, and I look forward to your prompt [134].

Kind regards,
Rei Matsumoto

questions.question_text: Can you please confirm that the room will have a balcony with a _____ of the sea, as promised?
questions.explanation: The collocation needed here is 'a view of' + noun.

questions.question_text: Can you also confirm that the hotel provides free internet access, and that the room _____ a safe?
questions.explanation: The future simple is the only tense that fits the meaning of this sentence.

questions.question_text: We understand that no deposit is required, but we need to make payment in full on arrival. _____

options.A: Your cooperation in this matter is appreciated.
options.B: We are looking forward to our stay in your hotel.
options.C: If you have any questions, please get in touch.
options.D: We accept all major credit cards.

questions.explanation: This sentence fits best as it aims to conclude the message in a positive way and logically follows the payment information.

questions.question_text: Thank you for your time, and I look forward to your prompt _____.
questions.explanation: Only the noun can be used after the adjective 'prompt'.

question_groups.passage_content:
Here at Corporate Vision, we make it our business to help your business. We are Canada's No.1 supplier of glasses and No.1 provider of eye tests. We have also been voted the country's most [135] opticians for the past ten years.

Contact our Account Management team today to discuss your eye care needs. Soon, you could be [136] the peace of mind that comes with knowing you are caring for your company's most important assets … its employees. Research shows that eye care benefits are highly valued by employees, and are an effective way to encourage staff loyalty. [137] If your employees use screens for periods of an hour or more continuously, then you must comply with Display Screen Regulations.

What's more, corporate client employees can take advantage of our exclusive Executive Club. Employees are entitled to three Executive Club coupons per year. Distribute the coupons [138] your staff, and they can be redeemed at any store.
question_groups.passage_title: Corporate Vision – The Eye Care Specialists

questions.question_text: We have also been voted the country's most _____ opticians for the past ten years.
questions.explanation: This adjective (meaning reliable) best completes the sentence.

questions.question_text: Soon, you could be _____ the peace of mind that comes with knowing you are caring for your company's most important assets.
questions.explanation: Here 'could be + -ing' is used to express possibility in the future.

questions.question_text: Research shows that eye care benefits are highly valued by employees, and are an effective way to encourage staff loyalty. _____

options.A: Laser eye surgery is also available.
options.B: Tell your staff now about this exciting opportunity.
options.C: On-site eye testing can be arranged.
options.D: Providing eye care is also a legal requirement.

questions.explanation: This sentence fits best as it closely relates to the following sentence that mentions Display Screen Regulations.

questions.question_text: Distribute the coupons _____ your staff, and they can be redeemed at any store.
questions.explanation: The preposition 'among' is used after 'distribute' to indicate sharing between a group.

question_groups.passage_content:
Work-related stress is an everyday part of most people's lives. That's the result of one recent survey of over 15,000 workers across ten countries. A staggering 70% of workers described their jobs as 'very stressful.' [139]

According to the U-Poll International survey, the most stressful of all occupations include police officer, firefighter, and commercial pilot. The statistics suggest that work-related stress affects men and women equally, [140] that older workers suffer more than younger people.

The poll found that 84% of respondents [141] high levels of stress at some point in their lives. Common causes of stress at work include large workloads, lack of support, weak management, bullying or harassment, and a poor working environment.

It seems that [142] no one is immune to stress. In Japan, the perceived threat from this 'silent killer' is so strong that the Japanese even have a word for sudden death as a result of overwork, 'karoushi.'
question_groups.passage_title: International Poll Shows Stress Affects Us All

questions.question_text: A staggering 70% of workers described their jobs as 'very stressful.' _____

options.A: The effects of stress in the workplace can be alarming.
options.B: However, the reasons remain largely unknown.
options.C: Reducing stress must be a priority.
options.D: Most respondents felt stressed to some degree.

questions.explanation: This sentence links with what has gone before and is a continuation of the survey findings.

questions.question_text: The statistics suggest that work-related stress affects men and women equally, _____ that older workers suffer more than younger people.
questions.explanation: The conjunction 'but' expresses the contrast between these two clauses.

questions.question_text: The poll found that 84% of respondents _____ high levels of stress at some point in their lives.
questions.explanation: The past simple is used to indicate finished time.

questions.question_text: It seems that _____ no one is immune to stress.
questions.explanation: Of these adverbs, only 'virtually' (meaning almost) fits the meaning of the sentence.

question_groups.passage_content:
To: All staff
From: Shirley Blaine
Re: Security issues
Date: September 14th

In recent weeks there have been a number of thefts from the premises. These include a client's notebook computer taken from Study Center 1, one of our new digital cameras that went [143] from the reception area, and Carol Miller's purse, which apparently was stolen yesterday from her desk while she was at lunch.

Obviously, this is an important matter and we have asked the police to investigate. In the meantime, I'd like to ask everyone to take particular care of your personal belongings, especially any valuable items (bags, wallets, cell phones, etc.). Always lock your offices when you leave, [144] it's only for a short time. We need to do everything we can to deter opportunistic thieves. Please be vigilant and report anyone [145] in a suspicious manner to Bob or Michael in Security immediately.

We also have an ultra-violet marker pen at reception, for you to mark items such as notebook computers and cell phones. This is very easy and quick to do, so please take advantage of this security option. [146]

questions.question_text: One of our new digital cameras that went _____ from the reception area.
questions.explanation: The collocation here is 'go missing' (meaning disappear).

questions.question_text: Always lock your offices when you leave, _____ it's only for a short time.
questions.explanation: This is the only conjunction that fits the sentence.

questions.question_text: Please be vigilant and report anyone _____ in a suspicious manner to Bob or Michael in Security immediately.
questions.explanation: The gerund is needed as the object of the sentence.

questions.question_text: Please take advantage of this security option. _____

options.A: Please be sure to return it after use.
options.B: Therefore, more pens will be purchased.
options.C: Thank you for joining the security team.
options.D: I will let you know when it is available.

questions.explanation: This sentence refers directly to the ultra-violet marker pen and logically completes the paragraph.

parts.title: Part 7 – Reading Comprehension
parts.description: Read the texts. For each question, decide which of the four answer choices: (A), (B), (C), or (D), best answers the question.

question_groups.passage_content: Celine Depardieu contacts Assistant Mandy P about order GH-35832/TW. She ordered 200 copies of 'English Student Plus' but received 250. The assistant apologizes, explains the company policy requires the 50 extra books to be returned with a pre-paid label, and offers a $50 credit note as compensation. A new invoice will be sent.
question_groups.passage_title: Online Chat Discussion – Book Order Issue

questions.question_text: At 3:34 P.M., what does Ms. Depardieu mean when she writes, 'That's a pain'?
questions.explanation: If something is 'a pain', it means it is very troublesome or inconvenient.

questions.question_text: What will Mandy P most likely do next?
questions.explanation: At 3:35 P.M. Mandy P writes she will 'send out a new invoice right away'. We can infer she will revise the bill.

question_groups.passage_content: Sue Morriss from Hertford Legal's Accounts Payable writes to Mark Jenkins at Thomson Inc. about an unpaid outstanding invoice (ref #5098). She apologizes, requests a hard copy of the invoice, and explains the department was recently reorganized. Dan Denapoli (previous contact) has transferred to Investments. Sue introduces herself as the new Relationship Manager.
question_groups.passage_title: E-mail – Request for Copy of Invoice (Hertford Legal)

questions.question_text: What does this e-mail confirm?
questions.explanation: The e-mail refers to an outstanding invoice and apologizes that this has been unpaid.

questions.question_text: Who is Dan Denapoli?

options.A: A worker in the Accounts Payable department
options.D: An accountant at the main office

questions.explanation: The e-mail says Dan Denapoli has transferred to the Investments division. He is a coworker at Hertford Legal.

questions.question_text: The word 'oversight' in paragraph 2, line 1, is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation: Here, the word 'oversight' means mistake or error.

question_groups.passage_content: Sue Watkins and Zoe Trent are meeting up. Zoe was held up at the office by Gavin. Zoe is on public transport (two more stops). They are going to a venue on Cowper Road with great reviews. Jason Pollard is featured there, whom both women admire for his role in 'Back Home'.
question_groups.passage_title: Text Message Chain – Sue Watkins & Zoe Trent

questions.question_text: At 8:38 P.M., what does Ms. Watkins mean when she writes, 'Tell me about it'?

options.A: She wants to hear more information.
options.C: She was unaware of the problem.
options.D: She expected Ms. Trent to be late.

questions.explanation: The phrase 'Tell me about it' is used to indicate you understand what someone is talking about because you have had a similar experience.

questions.question_text: Who most likely is Jason Pollard?
questions.explanation: The women mention great reviews and Jason Pollard is in it. Sue mentions he was fantastic in 'Back Home'. We can infer 'Back Home' is a movie and Jason Pollard is an actor.

question_groups.passage_content: Agenda for the Steering Group meeting on Monday, October 14 at 7:30 P.M. The previous meeting was September 16, and the next meeting is November 12. The chairperson is Mr. R. Lee. The agenda includes action items on fundraising, student engagement, diversity, tuition/fees, and the Africa Project, among others.
question_groups.passage_title: Chorley County Community College – Steering Group Agenda

questions.question_text: How often does the Steering Group most likely meet?
questions.explanation: The last meeting was September 16, the current meeting is October 14, and the next is November 12 – approximately monthly.

questions.question_text: What was NOT discussed at this meeting?

options.A: Plans to raise money for the college
options.B: Efforts to encourage more students to get involved
options.C: Decisions made at the last meeting
options.D: Ways to promote the college's online courses

questions.explanation: Fundraising (A), promoting greater student engagement (B), and approval of matters from the previous meeting (C) are all on the agenda. Online courses are not mentioned.

question_groups.passage_content: Memo from Cheryl Baker (Marketing Manager) dated March 19, announcing two new Marketing team appointments: Leanne Riggins (starts March 25, Houston office, account manager) and Kevin Rose (starts first of next month, three days a week until October 1, marketing assistant, recently graduated). Staff are asked to help the newcomers settle in. Specific tasks assigned to Brad (login/access), Naomi (security clearance), Claire (badges), and Molly (AccountPro setup).
question_groups.passage_title: MEMO – Developing the Marketing Team (Hopkins Institute)

questions.question_text: What is the purpose of the memo?

options.B: To notify employees of new work policies

questions.explanation: The purpose of the memo, stated in the opening line, is to announce two further appointments.

questions.question_text: How much time does Brad have to prepare for Kevin's arrival?

options.B: Until the end of the week

questions.explanation: The memo states Kevin will begin on the first of next month. The date of the memo is March 19, so Brad has less than two weeks (about 12 days).

questions.question_text: What can be inferred about The Hopkins Institute?

options.A: It is a Market Research company.
options.B: It is expanding its marketing operations.
options.C: It needs help to develop its marketing projects.
options.D: It relies heavily on marketing technology.

questions.explanation: Both new appointments are additions to the Marketing team, implying the institute is growing its marketing operations.

question_groups.passage_content: Mr. S. Bernstein has booked parking for a Silver Honda (license plate 674 NDA, 2 passengers) at Perfect Parking Park & Ride, Vancouver. Meeting at 2:00 P.M. on Jan 15 for flight BA015. Return at 1:35 P.M. on Jan 28 on flight BA014. Total cost: $280 (paid). Cancellations within 24 hours incur a $40 charge. No-shows are non-refundable.

questions.question_text: How long has Mr. Bernstein reserved to park his car at the Perfect Parking lot?
questions.explanation: The booking begins Jan 15 and ends Jan 28 = 14 days.

questions.question_text: How much money will Mr. Bernstein receive if he cancels his booking on the day of departure?
questions.explanation: For cancellations within 24 hours there is a $40 charge. The total charge is $280. Therefore he will receive $280 - $40 = $240.

question_groups.passage_content: Liam O'Toole (Conference Reservations, Bellevue Hotel) writes to Andrei Ivanski about a provisional booking of the Main Conference Room for May 23–27 (1:30–4:30 P.M.), total cost £800 including a 15% off-peak (weekday) discount. [1] Mr. Ivanski must provide public liability insurance. [2] Catering prices are being revised and will increase after May 9. [3] He must complete and return the attached catering price list and facilities order form before the event. [4] He must read and sign the terms and conditions.
question_groups.passage_title: E-mail – Bellevue Hotel Room Rental Details

questions.question_text: In which position marked [1], [2], [3], and [4] does the following sentence best belong? 'This is also inclusive of all taxes.'
questions.explanation: This sentence fits best after [1] as it follows on from the sentences discussing the total cost.

questions.question_text: What is implied about the rental Mr. Ivanski has made?

options.A: It would cost more to hold the event on the weekend.
options.B: The Main Conference Room is the only suitable room.
options.C: All supplementary charges are already included in the quote.
options.D: The hotel will provide comprehensive insurance coverage.

questions.explanation: The first paragraph states these are all weekdays and the rate includes a 15% off-peak discount, implying weekend rates would be higher.

questions.question_text: The word 'records' in paragraph 4, line 3, is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation: Here, the word 'records' means files or paperwork.

questions.question_text: How many documents does Mr. Ivanski need to supply?
questions.explanation: Mr. Ivanski needs to send: (1) a copy of his public liability insurance, (2) completed catering price list and facilities order form, and (3) the original signed terms and conditions.

question_groups.passage_content: Article by Kyong Lee about the global recession's impact on various industries. Traditional industries (aerospace, textiles, shipbuilding) declined by up to 40%. [1] [2] Companies making microchip manufacturing equipment saw healthy sales growth, especially TWN Technologies (sales doubled in 4 quarters). Drivers include demand for smartphones, tablets, wireless gaming, mobile video, in-car navigation, and telecommunications. DRAM memory prices rose 17%. [3] [4] Dr. Jane Murthy (Mann Research) says microchip demand typically signals the start of a recovery.

questions.question_text: What is suggested about hi-tech companies?

options.A: Most remain badly affected by the recession.
options.B: Some are expected to recover in the near future.
options.C: They have all reported poor results for the last year.
options.D: Certain sectors are enjoying strong growth.

questions.explanation: The article reports healthy sales growth among microchip manufacturers. Certain hi-tech sectors are doing well.

questions.question_text: In which position marked [1], [2], [3], and [4] does the following sentence best belong? 'However, this grim picture is showing signs of change.'
questions.explanation: This sentence fits best at [2] as it introduces a paragraph showing more healthy economic outlook, contrasting with the previous paragraph about decline.

questions.question_text: Which of these is NOT mentioned as a cause for increased demand for microchips?

options.D: Growth in sales of desktop computers

questions.explanation: Online gaming (A), in-car navigation systems (B), and smartphones (C) are all mentioned. Only (D) desktop computers is not mentioned.

question_groups.passage_content: Services guide for the Tenth Annual Conference (June 2–5, IEC International Exhibition Center, Berlin). Services include: Infodesk (volunteers at 10 locations, main Infodesk in Hall 2), Coat Check (near Hall 1 entrance, permanently staffed), Catering (Breakfast Bar in D Building from 7:30 A.M., Cafeteria in Central Plaza, catering vans from 10:30 A.M.), Physical Security (guards patrol, staff in red T-shirts), Lost & Found (main Infodesk), First Aid (follow signs in Hall 4). Requests: do not leave valuables unattended, comply with fire safety signs, recycle waste.
question_groups.passage_title: InterContinental Trade Show – Services During the Event

questions.question_text: Who would be most interested in this information?

options.A: Attendees at the InterContinental Trade Show
options.C: Managers at the International Exhibition Center
options.D: Staff responsible for security at the event

questions.explanation: The information covers various services that may be of interest to conference attendees.

questions.question_text: Where should you go to reclaim any lost property?
questions.explanation: Lost property is kept at the main Infodesk, which is located in Hall 2.

questions.question_text: Which of the following topics is NOT covered?
questions.explanation: Physical security (A), fire safety (C), and recycling (D) are all mentioned. Vehicle security (B) is not mentioned.

questions.question_text: The word 'valuables' in the 'Some requests' section, is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation: Here, the word 'valuables' means important items or things of value.

question_groups.passage_content: Invoice #3498FD dated June 4. Visit on May 29 to address false alarms. Two smoke detectors found: both stripped, cleaned, and tested. The lounge detector failed (false alarm, unknown cause) – replaced with a new optical unit (FX114, $80). Landing unit verified working correctly. Labor: $95. Total: $185.50 (incl. 9% sales tax). Payment due within 14 days.
question_groups.passage_title: A1 Alarm Maintenance Invoice – Mr. James, Ames, IA

questions.question_text: Why did Mr. James contact A1 Alarm Maintenance?

options.A: The smoke detectors were not working properly.
options.B: He wanted a new smoke alarm system installed.
options.C: One of the smoke alarms did not meet international standards.
options.D: The smoke alarm system needed routine maintenance.

questions.explanation: The invoice refers to reports of false alarms, indicating the detectors were not working properly.

questions.question_text: Who most likely is Tim Edwards?

options.A: The owner of A1 Alarm Maintenance
options.D: An invoice clerk for A1 Alarm Maintenance

questions.explanation: Mr. Edwards' name follows the report of work done, so it can be inferred that he carried out the work – he is an alarm maintenance engineer.

questions.question_text: By which date should payment in full be made?
questions.explanation: The last paragraph states 'Terms strictly 14 days'. The date of the letter is June 4, so the payment deadline is June 18.

questions.question_text: Which service was NOT carried out at the property?
questions.explanation: The unit on the landing was working correctly, so a repair was not necessary. Both units were cleaned (A). A new optical unit (C) was fitted in the lounge. After replacement, only the landing unit was verified working – both detectors were not fully retested.

question_groups.passage_content:
Paramount Hotels ad: Luxury locations in the US (Imperial Hotel LA, Boathouse Hotel Maine, Old Stables Hotel Chicago, Park Hotel New York). Rates from $120 in August, 10% discount for online bookings, free fruit basket for Frequent Travel Club members. Special Offer: Stay two nights in July – get free breakfast.

Reservation form filled by Thomas Whitly (Atlanta, GA): Park Hotel, single room, 1 person, check-in 7/23, check-out 7/25 (2 nights). Not a Frequent Travel Club member. Form being sent by mail.
question_groups.passage_title: Paramount Hotels Advertisement & Reservation Form (Thomas Whitly)

questions.question_text: Which city does Mr. Whitly plan to visit?
questions.explanation: Mr. Whitly has reserved the Park Hotel, which the ad states is in New York.

questions.question_text: How is Mr. Whitly making this hotel reservation?
questions.explanation: The form asks to 'send this page to us with your deposit to confirm your reservation'. The form is completed by hand and is not on a website.

questions.question_text: Which of the following will Mr. Whitly qualify to receive?

options.A: A 10 percent discount on his bill
options.B: Breakfast each morning free of charge
options.C: A special room rate of $120 per night

questions.explanation: The ad states 'Stay two nights in July and get breakfast absolutely free'. Mr. Whitly's booking is for July 23–25 (2 nights in July). He qualifies for free breakfast.

questions.question_text: In the advertisement, the word 'unique' in paragraph 1, line 1, is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation: Here, the word 'unique' means distinctive or exclusive.

questions.question_text: Which of the following characteristics of Paramount Hotels does the advertisement NOT mention?
questions.explanation: The ad states they are right in the heart of unique locations (A), provide fine dining (B), and have rooms at great prices (C). Recently refurbished (D) is not mentioned.

question_groups.passage_content:
Article about a panel of experts at the Wellington Business Forum 'Festival of Ideas': Dr. Philippe Gaston (Hendrix Associates) predicts millions of new consumers in global markets. Prof. Marvin Klysters (Wellington Business Institute) agrees that disposable incomes in emerging economies will rise. Jason Foulkes (NZ Shipping Association) is excited about export opportunities. Dr. Carole Sherringham (colleague of Prof. Klysters) cites statistical data suggesting Asia's economy could grow by 40% in 5 years, potentially overtaking the US by 2030. The festival lasts until Friday 27th at the West Road Theater.

Final Week Calendar: 'A World of Opportunity' (Tue Oct 24, Dr. Xavier Hernandes), 'Emerging Markets' (Thu Oct 26, Mr. Ian Sissons, NZ Times), 'Environmental Market Opportunities' (Fri Oct 27, Prof. Lee Kwok, Green Matters Inc., Auckland).
question_groups.passage_title: Developing Global Markets – Wellington Business Forum 'Festival of Ideas'

questions.question_text: Where will Professor Lee Kwok's presentation be held?
questions.explanation: According to the article, all talks are held in the West Road Theater.

questions.question_text: Which economy is expected to expand by nearly half in the next five years?
questions.explanation: The article states Asia's economy could grow by as much as 40% in the next five years.

questions.question_text: Where does Dr. Sherringham work?
questions.explanation: Dr. Sherringham is a colleague of Prof. Klysters, who works at Wellington Business Institute.

questions.question_text: In the article, the word 'leading' in paragraph 1, line 3, is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation: Here, the word 'leading' means renowned or top.

questions.question_text: Which speaker does NOT refer to statistics to support his or her argument?
questions.explanation: Dr. Hernandes mentions facts and figures, Dr. Sherringham provides statistical data, and Prof. Klysters references figures. Mr. Ian Sissons does not specifically refer to statistics.

question_groups.passage_content:
Five job ads: (1) Bilingual Call Center Assistant (Total Call Insurance) – min. 2 years call center experience, Spanish/English bilingual preferred. (2) Customer Service Representative (Mellistone Edwards) – high school diploma required. (3) Administrative Assistant (Campbelltown Medical Center, part-time) – excellent communication, 60 wpm typing, French useful, no stated experience requirement. (4) Clerical Assistant (Deutsche Werkzeuge GmbH) – extensive admin background, fluent in German. (5) Accounting Assistant (JV Watson) – bilingual German/English, accounting experience essential, accounting degree required.

E-mail 1: Claire Robertson applies for the Bilingual Call Center Assistant position. She has a high school diploma, 4 years call center experience (1 year at Etrusco Financial Services, part-time). She's studying for a Certificate in General Insurance.

E-mail 2: Jefferson Waters (Team Leader, InvoPro) recommends Claire Robertson. She worked at InvoPro's Call Center for 2 years before joining Etrusco. Wholeheartedly recommends her.
question_groups.passage_title: Job Advertisements – Call Center & Office Positions

questions.question_text: Which position does NOT require previous experience?
questions.explanation: The advertisement for Administrative Assistant mentions no requirement for previous experience – only communication skills, typing speed, and French (useful but not essential).

questions.question_text: What is indicated about Claire Robertson?
questions.explanation: Claire Robertson writes 'I am currently attending a night class that leads to the Certificate in General Insurance'.

questions.question_text: As well as Total Call Insurance, which organization is Claire Robertson also qualified to apply to?
questions.explanation: She has a high school diploma and 4 years of relevant experience. She does not mention speaking French, German, or having accounting or admin qualifications. Mellistone Edwards (Customer Service Representative) requires only a high school diploma.

questions.question_text: How long ago did Mr. Waters first meet Ms. Robertson?
questions.explanation: Mr. Waters says Claire worked at InvoPro for two years before moving to Etrusco. Claire says she has been at Etrusco for a year. So Waters first met Claire 2 + 1 = 3 years ago.

questions.question_text: In the e-mail from Mr. Waters, the word 'wholeheartedly' in paragraph 1, line 5, is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation: 'Wholeheartedly' means with all one's heart (i.e., totally/completely).

question_groups.passage_content:
CleanCut Knives website: Products include 7-inch chef's knife ($59), 8-inch butcher's cleaver ($175), 9-inch bread knife ($79), 11-inch carving knife ($95). All knives have stainless steel blades (rust-resistant), hand-wash only, lifetime warranty.

Order #5755: 1 x kitchen knife (7-inch chef's knife), $59.00. Tax (8%): $4.72. Standard shipping (free). Total: $63.72.

E-mail from Douglas Long (Senior Chef, Grand Metropolitan Hotel) dated June 15: The knife arrived but his credit card was debited over $85. He believes he was charged for a $79 knife (bread knife price) instead of what he ordered. Asks for immediate rectification.

questions.question_text: What is NOT indicated about the knives?

options.B: They should be washed by hand.

questions.explanation: Sharpening is not mentioned. The knives have a lifetime warranty (A), are not dishwasher-safe (B), and have high carbon stainless steel blades which won't rust (C).

questions.question_text: Which size knife did the customer buy?
questions.explanation: The knife the customer bought cost $59. We can see from the web page that this is the cost of a 7-inch chef's knife.

questions.question_text: What will the hotel kitchen most likely use the knife for?
questions.explanation: The 7-inch chef's knife is ideal for cutting and slicing fruit and vegetables, and for all the smaller kitchen jobs – such as chopping herbs.

questions.question_text: What does Mr. Long imply in the e-mail?

options.B: He was charged the price of a bread knife.
options.C: The package arrived later than agreed.
options.D: The parcel did not contain a delivery note.

questions.explanation: Douglas Long writes he was charged over $85 and 'it looks like you charged me for a $79 knife'. From the web page, the $79 knife is the 9-inch bread knife.

questions.question_text: In the e-mail, the word 'rectify' in line 3 is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation: The verb 'rectify' means to correct or put something right.

question_groups.passage_content:
Boot Camp notice: Two-day military-style fitness program. Activities may include circuit training, bodyweight training, boxing/boxercise, cycling, hiking, running, power yoga, team games, aerobics. Massage/relaxation available for an extra charge – everything else is included.

Comment by Lisa Liu: She enjoyed the weekend. Doesn't think she lost weight (ate too much). Improved technique. Plans to return before her August wedding. Thanks Matt (most patient with her – she was the slowest).

E-mail from Logan Dexter to Fitness Instructors: 25 participants signed up. Saturday morning: Brad leads boxercise and obstacle course; Teri leads aerobics and basketball; Kris leads circuit training. Saturday afternoon: Teri leads hiking, Matt organizes a 10-mile bike ride, Logan takes a long run. Charmaine is unavailable – her sessions cannot run as no one else is qualified.
question_groups.passage_title: Weekend Fitness Boot Camp – Notice, Comment & Instructor E-mail

questions.question_text: What is indicated about the boot camp?

options.B: The program is the same each boot camp.
options.D: Some activities must be paid for.

questions.explanation: The notice states there is an extra charge for massage and relaxation treatments – these are the only activities you will have to pay for.

questions.question_text: Who is NOT scheduled to lead a session on Saturday afternoon?
questions.explanation: Logan will take anyone interested on a long run, Matt organizes a bike ride, and Teri leads the hiking group on Saturday afternoon. Kris is only mentioned for circuit training in the morning.

questions.question_text: What can be inferred about Charmaine?

options.A: She leads the power yoga sessions.
options.B: She usually works on Sundays only.
options.C: She is currently away on vacation.

questions.explanation: The email says Charmaine's sessions cannot run as no-one else is qualified. Power yoga is advertised but not assigned to any other instructor, suggesting Charmaine normally leads it.

questions.question_text: Which advertised activity did NOT take place on Saturday at this boot camp?
questions.explanation: Logan does not mention bodyweight training in his e-mail. Teri led aerobics (A), Brad led the obstacle course (B), and Teri led basketball which is a team game (D).

questions.question_text: What is true about Lisa Liu?

options.A: She lost weight at the boot camp.
options.B: She went cycling on the Saturday.
options.C: She attended the event twice before.

questions.explanation: Lisa thanks Matt, who was patient with her. Matt organized a ten-mile bike ride on Saturday afternoon. So Lisa was in Matt's cycling group and went cycling.
